title: Oppenheimer and 1954 Atomic Energy Act
summary: This interview segment discusses the historical connections between Oppenheimer, the Manhattan Project, and the development of atomic secrecy laws. It covers how the 1954 Atomic Energy Act created a classification system for radiological materials that operates outside normal oversight.

acronyms.definition: Federal agency that emerged from the Atomic Energy Commission to oversee nuclear and energy matters

events.description: Condon writes the McMahon Secrecy Act, establishing key protocols for nuclear trade secret secrecy
The Atomic Energy Commission is formed, creating special classification for radiological materials

key_claims.claim: Condon and Oppenheimer studied together in Germany in the 1930s
Condon fell out with Leslie Groves over compartmentalization and secrecy issues six weeks into the Manhattan Project
Under the Atomic Energy Act, anything emitting radiological properties is classified as 'born secret' and falls under DOE classification free from oversight
